themes.label: AI Hardware Supercycle
themes.narrative: The AI capex bubble is deflating. Post-AVGO earnings, stocks sold off on a minor guidance sub-line miss. The infrastructure spend is peaking. Hyperscalers will cut capex.
themes.dataAnchor: AVGO Q2 2026 (June 3): revenue $22.2B (+48% YoY, beat estimate of $22.04B). AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY). Q3 guided $29.4B total (+84% YoY), AI revenue guided >200% YoY. Order book confirmed >$70B. The stock fell -3% post-earnings on a minor infrastructure software miss — not on AI hardware. That is the data-narrative gap.
themes.counterRead: The market read the minor software miss as a capex-peak signal. The AI hardware data disagrees: $10.8B AI revenue in Q2 followed by >200% guided growth in Q3 is not a plateau. The custom ASIC order book of >$70B from Google, Meta, and Apple represents years of revenue already contracted. The -3% dip is mispricing. The entry window is open now before Q3 results confirm the acceleration.
themes.watchFor: AVGO Q3 results (Sept 2026): AI revenue confirm >$12B quarter. Any hyperscaler capex cut in quarterly guidance would change this read.

themes.label: Defense / Nuclear Backlog
themes.narrative: Nuclear stocks are speculative SMR plays. Government defense stocks are interest-rate sensitive and tied to discretionary budget decisions.
themes.dataAnchor: BWXT Q1 2026: $860M revenue, FY guided $3.75B+. Backlog $8.65B (+77% YoY) as of March 2026. $1.29B long-lead US Naval Nuclear Propulsion award + $165M Ford-class aircraft carrier reactor contract. US naval nuclear reactor production is mandated by federal law — not discretionary. 60% of remaining backlog converts to revenue by end of 2027. Deutsche Bank upgraded. Zero AI disruption risk.
themes.counterRead: BWXT is not an SMR speculation — it is a legally mandated defense supplier with $8.65B in confirmed, contracted backlog. The narrative lumps it with speculative nuclear names like OKLO. The data shows 2.3x annual revenue in backlog that was already signed before today. The market is pricing it as a cyclical industrial; it behaves more like a utility with guaranteed revenue.
themes.watchFor: US defense budget sequestration cut >20% (would reduce naval nuclear orders). SMR commercial order flow would be upside catalyst, not base case.

themes.label: SaaSpocalypse — who survives
themes.narrative: Agentic AI (January 2026 breakout) is destroying the seat-based SaaS revenue model. IGV software ETF -21% YTD. All enterprise software is going to zero. AI agents replace every CRM seat, every monitoring tool, every workflow.
themes.dataAnchor: The macro is real: IGV -21% YTD, -30% from September 2025 peak. BUT the narrative is painting every software company with the same brush. DDOG Q1 2026: +32% YoY to $1.01B (first $1B quarter), FCF $289M at 29% margin, four consecutive quarters of accelerating growth — raised guide $240M. AI generates MORE logs and metrics, not fewer. HUBS Q1 +23% but management itself warned on AI transition to seat-based CRM — that risk is real and self-disclosed. NOW Q1 +22%, $1.67B FCF, 44% margin — workflow automation embedded too deeply to displace quickly.
themes.counterRead: The blanket SaaS sell-off creates separation between beneficiaries and casualties. DDOG benefits from AI proliferation (more AI workloads = more observability data). HUBS is genuinely at risk (seat-based CRM is what AI agents cannibalize first). The opportunity is in companies where AI is additive to revenue, not destructive. DDOG's accelerating growth while its sector declines is the signal.
themes.watchFor: HUBS Q2 2026: whether seat expansion stabilizes or accelerates the decline. DDOG Q2 2026: whether the $1B+ quarterly revenue pace holds and guide raises again.

themes.label: Industrial / Infrastructure Rotation
themes.narrative: Industrials are expensive after a multi-year run. The easy money in power infrastructure has been made. VRT has had its moment.
themes.dataAnchor: VRT Q4 2025 orders: +252% YoY. FCF $1.9B. $15B order book. FY2026 organic growth guided 27-29%. RSI at 36.2. Down -14.8% last month. 22 of 26 covering banks rate Buy or Strong Buy. The macro rotation INTO industrials/energy/materials from tech/SaaS is active: Energy and Industrials are the YTD leaders while IGV -21%. The VRT dislocation vs. the industrial macro trend is the gap.
themes.counterRead: VRT at 49x forward P/E carries real downside (-30-40%) if hyperscaler capex decelerates. The bear case is not wrong — it just requires a specific scenario. Against that: the $15B order book provides 18+ months of revenue visibility that doesn't evaporate in one quarter. The industrial macro rotation is institutional and persistent. The risk-reward improves the further it corrects.
themes.watchFor: VRT Q2 2026: order rate and margin. Any two consecutive quarters of hyperscaler capex guidance cuts would validate the bear case.

themes.label: Credit / Macro Posture
themes.narrative: Recession is coming. Rate hikes broke credit. Consumer is weakening.
themes.dataAnchor: HY OAS: 2.75% — historically tight. Not pricing credit stress. Yield curve: 2s10s steepening (+41bp) — historically a recovery signal, not a recession signal. Fed at 3.50-3.75% with cuts in progress. The Kostolany Phase D read remains intact: rates down, credit available, economy stabilizing. SPY above 200d MA.
themes.counterRead: No significant divergence between the macro narrative and the credit data. HY spreads widen before recessions — they are tightening. This is a multiple compression event in growth stocks (duration sensitivity to real rates), not a credit event. The posture stays: Hold and sharpen. Preserve capital in defensible names, sharpen positions in verified dislocations.
themes.watchFor: HY OAS above 4.0% — credit starting to price stress, posture shifts defensive. SPY breaks 200d MA — structural breakdown, re-evaluate all positions.

strategyPosture: Sharpen and deploy — but only in names with verified data. AVGO post-earnings dip, BWXT on confirmed backlog, VRT on industrial rotation. The capital preservation filter is strict: no name that the data does not directly support. Hold existing positions. No defensive action required — credit is clean and the 200d MA is intact.
whereWaveBuilds: AI hardware (AVGO post-Q2 dip before Q3 confirms +$29.4B). Defense nuclear infrastructure (BWXT backlog converting through 2027). AI-native observability (DDOG accelerating while sector rotates). The wave does not build in seat-based SaaS or pre-commercial speculative names.
watchFor: AVGO Q3 Sept 2026: AI revenue confirm >$12B — validates the $29.4B guide and post-Q2 entry thesis
BWXT additional naval contract awards — accelerates backlog above $10B threshold
DDOG Q2 2026: revenue acceleration continues — second quarter above $1B with raised guide would confirm AI-monitoring thesis
HY OAS above 4.0% — posture shifts to capital preservation mode immediately
Any two consecutive hyperscaler capex guidance cuts — would invalidate the VRT and AVGO infrastructure theses simultaneously
